response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an accomplished senior software engineer with strong full-stack and backend credentials, but the Principal Performance Architect role demands deep hardware expertise — SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator optimization, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design for silicon — none of which appear in his resume. His performance work is limited to software-layer optimizations (async I/O, low-latency microservices) and his AI exposure is confined to agentic tooling and MCP frameworks, not deep-learning workload analysis or neural network inference optimization. The skill gap is fundamental, not incremental.
strengths: 15+ years of continuous software engineering experience across diverse domains, satisfying the Bachelor's+8yr experience threshold
Demonstrated performance-conscious engineering: high-throughput microservices handling millions of HTTP requests per second at Sociomantic using async I/O, caching, and fibers
Breadth of low-level exposure including C/C++ at National Instruments (LabWindows CVI), embedded systems, and hardware-adjacent work with robotic arms
concerns: Zero SoC or IP modeling experience — the primary technical requirement of the role — with no evidence of silicon design, RTL simulation, or architectural modeling
No background in AI/ML hardware: no GPU architecture, no SIMD/MIMD, no deep neural network training or inference optimization
Role requires principal-level IC work on cloud hardware infrastructure for AI accelerators; candidate's trajectory is firmly in web/backend software product engineering

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware architecture experience
No AI/deep-learning workload or GPU architecture background
